label: Braze
description: Connect to Braze to manage users, send messages, and trigger campaigns via the Braze REST API.

form.title: Connect Braze
form.subtitle: Paste your Braze REST API key and endpoint URL to give Anton access to your Braze workspace.

methods.label: REST API Key
methods.description: Paste your Braze REST API key and your instance's REST endpoint URL. The simplest and most direct way to connect — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Braze REST API Key

Braze uses per-workspace API keys and per-instance REST endpoints. You'll need both.

### Step 1 — Find your REST endpoint

1. Log in to your Braze dashboard.
2. Check the URL in your browser. Your instance determines your endpoint, e.g.:
   - `https://rest.iad-01.braze.com` for US-01
   - `https://rest.iad-02.braze.com` for US-02
   - `https://rest.fra-01.braze.eu` for EU-01
3. Full list: [Braze API endpoints](https://www.braze.com/docs/api/basics/#endpoints).

### Step 2 — Create a REST API key

1. In your Braze dashboard, go to **Settings → APIs and Identifiers → API Keys**.
2. Click **Create New API Key**.
3. Give it a descriptive name like `Anton`.
4. Select the permission scopes Anton needs (e.g. `users.track`, `campaigns.list`, `messages.send`, etc.).
5. Optionally restrict by IP allowlist for extra security.
6. Click **Save** — the key is shown immediately. Copy it.

### Step 3 — Paste into Anton

1. Paste the REST endpoint URL into **REST Endpoint URL** below (include `https://`).
2. Paste the API key into **REST API Key** below.
3. Click **Submit**.

### Notes

- API keys are workspace-scoped. If you have multiple workspaces, create a key in each and add a separate connector per workspace.
- Keys can be rotated or deleted under **Settings → APIs and Identifiers** at any time. Re-paste the new key into Anton after rotation.
- Braze does not show the full key after creation — copy it before closing the modal.

fields.name: rest_endpoint_url
fields.label: REST Endpoint URL
fields.description: The REST API base URL for your Braze instance. Find it at braze.com/docs/api/basics/#endpoints or infer it from your dashboard URL.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: REST API Key
fields.description: Found in Braze dashboard under Settings → APIs and Identifiers → API Keys. Copy it immediately after creation — it is not shown again in full.
